title: ficus filicauda var. longipes
intro: Ficus filicauda var. longipes, also known as the Long-stemmed Fig, is a variety of fig tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive long stems and figs. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections. The Long-stemmed Fig is said to provide various health benefits, including improved digestive health, relief from fever and cough, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are commonly used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues and other ailments.
